---
title: "*The Vapor Compression Cycle Reference Page*"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
::: {.callout-important title="Still Under Construction!"}
This web page is just a bare outline. The complete details have not been filled in yet.
:::
# Intro to *The Vapor Compression Cycle*

Main title explanation

## Revenge of the Fridge: Vapor Compression Strikes Back 

Video 1 explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/SN27SxDt0G4?si=JV0ibrl4Zg5B8zRp" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

## Modeling the Vapor Compression Cycle in DWSIM 

Video  explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/nWOz-Y7XsbQ?si=80tsMdSOaeHi01kO" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

- [Vapor Compression Cycle Model](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/entropy/VaporCompressionDWSIMCalc.ods)
- [DWSIM Vapor Compression Model](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/entropy/VapCompCalc.dwxmz)

## Definitions

[Refrigerator](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Refrigerator)
: A device that pumps heat from an internal cold space to the ambient.

[Heat Pump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heat_pump)
: A device that pumps heat from an external cold space to an internal warm space.

[Vapor Compression Cycle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vapor-compression_refrigeration)
: A thermodynamic refrigeration cycle consisting of four steps:<br/>1. An isobaric heating at low temperature<br/>2. An isentropic expansion<br/>3. An isobaric cooling at high temperature<br/>4. An isenthalpic expansion</br/>It usually involves phase transitions during the heating and cooling stages.

[Refrigerant](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Refrigerant)
: A fluid used in a vapor compression cycle. Common refrigerants are R-134a, ammonia, and propane (R-290).

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### The video previous to the previous video (i.e., two videos back)

**Cycle Wars: The Power Awakens**<br/>*Listing the common Air-Standard cycles, and modeling the Air-Standard Carnot cycle*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/M_p3gb79isg), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/AirStandardPowerCyclesInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/122-AirStandardCyclesPart1Visuals.qmd)

### The previous video (i.e., one video back)

**Cycle Wars: The Rise of Otto Cycles**<br/>*Modeling the Otto, Diesel, Brayton, Turbojet, Ericsson, & Stirling Cycles*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/jJaDQM8qjgU), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/AirStandardPowerCyclesInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/123-AirStandardCyclesPart2Visuals.qmd)

### The next video (i.e., one video forward)

**The Law is a Thief: It Destroys Exergy and Laughs in Your Face Part 1**<br/>*The Combined 1^st^ and 2^nd^ Laws*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/02S9SOGRBWU), [Info Page](../../balances/exergy/Combined1stAnd2ndLawsVideoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/exergy/129-IdealLostWorkVisuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**The Law is a Thief: It Destroys Exergy and Laughs in Your Face Part 2**<br/>*Example of The Combined 1^st^ and 2^nd^ Laws*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/ZAdAC4OccmI), [Info Page](../../balances/exergy/Combined1stAnd2ndLawsVideoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/exergy/130-IdealLostWorkExampleVisuals.qmd)
